slides.title: Feeling *in control* may add years
slides.sub: The Whitehall studies found that a sense of control over daily life predicts longer survival. Here is why it matters.

slides.title: The Whitehall effect
slides.body: British civil servants with less control over their work had *worse health* and shorter lives, even at the same income.

slides.title: Control quiets the stress system
slides.body: Feeling in charge of your day *can lower* chronic stress signaling, the slow-burn cortisol that wears the body down over time.

slides.title: Less strain, longer life
slides.body: Persistent stress is linked to higher blood pressure and inflammation. A greater sense of agency *may help* offset that load.

slides.title: Control starts small
slides.body: You cannot run everything. But choosing your sleep, meals and movement gives you *real, daily levers* you can actually pull.

slides.title: Seeing your own data helps
slides.body: Clarity is calming. When the numbers are visible, the day feels *less random* and more like something you steer.

slides.title: Put your day *back in your hands*.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores your sleep, food, movement and steps into one daily Health Score, so the things that protect you feel within reach.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
More control over your day is not just less stressful. It may help you live longer. 🌿

The Whitehall studies linked a sense of agency to lower chronic stress and better survival. Small, steady choices around sleep, food and movement are levers you actually hold. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns those choices into one daily score → link in bio.
